message:
fix(types): match SQLite's %!.15g float-to-text formatting

SQLite's printf uses the `%!.15g` format specifier when converting REAL
values to TEXT, which guarantees a decimal point is always present. Our
to_text() and Display impl used Rust's default `{f}` format, which
omits the decimal point for whole-number floats (e.g. 120 instead of
120.0). This caused conformance failures wherever float values were
compared as text, since the output was indistinguishable from INTEGER.

Add format_sqlite_float() that ensures a `.0` suffix for whole numbers
while correctly handling NaN, Inf, and fractional values. Wire it into
both to_text() and Display.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
